Friday Fifteen: June 15th Edition

June 15, 2012

Each Friday I am going to attempt to give you high quality reading material for the weekend, in the form of fifteen links from the previous week.

Here we go – I hope you enjoy:

Technology/Business/Productivity

  • Research Shows That the Smarter People Are, the More Susceptible They Are to Cognitive Bias

  • What America Spends On Groceries

  • The Most Important Skill to Master


  • Turn Your Email into the Best Social Network You’ll Ever Use


  • The Three-Day Monk Syndrome


  • When Friction is Fiction


  • Three Little Habits to Find Focus


    • </ul>

    Politics

    • The Money Crisis: How Citizens United Undermines Our Elections and the Supreme Court


    • For such an anti-business president, Obama’s got a pretty pro-business record


    • How Americans have become more polarized, in two charts

    • Politifact, Politifact, Who Is The Truthiest Of Them All?


    • On 'This Week,' Paul Krugman Destroys Romney Surrogate, Calls Ryan Plan a 'Fraud'


    • This Republican Economy


    • They Are Both Keynesians Now
